summary: Typosquat of @metamask/sdk exfiltrates wallet keys, SSH keys, and secrets on install
details: @wagni_bot/metemask-sdk is a one-character typosquat of @metamask/sdk (description: 'Unofficial metemask-sdk SDK'; index.js exports an empty object). The package's only real code is postinstall.js, which runs automatically on `npm install` via scripts.postinstall. It scans the current working directory, home directory, and crypto-wallet paths (.ethereum,.solana,.bitcoin, Library/Ethereum) for files matching wallet/keystore/seed/mnemonic/.pem/id_rsa patterns, greps file contents for Ethereum (0x+64hex) and BTC WIF private keys and BIP-39 seed phrases, reads ~/.ssh/ private keys, and dumps environment variables matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/AWS. Harvested contents plus hostname, username, and cwd are HTTP POSTed in plaintext to hardcoded bare IP http://107.161.90.180:7777. The typosquat name targets exactly the wallet secrets a MetaMask developer would have locally.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
